principles.text: Rule No. 1: Never lose money. Rule No. 2: Never forget Rule No. 1.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Be fearful when others are greedy, and greedy when others are fearful.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: It's far better to buy a wonderful company at a fair price than a fair company at a wonderful price.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Our favorite holding period is forever.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Price is what you pay. Value is what you get.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: The stock market is a device for transferring money from the impatient to the patient.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Only when the tide goes out do you discover who's been swimming naked.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Risk comes from not knowing what you're doing.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Wide diversification is only required when investors do not understand what they are doing.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Never invest in a business you cannot understand.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: The most important thing in investing is knowing your circle of competence and staying within it.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Time is the friend of the wonderful company, the enemy of the mediocre.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: If you aren't willing to own a stock for ten years, don't even think about owning it for ten minutes.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Opportunities come infrequently. When it rains gold, put out the bucket, not the thimble.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: The best investment you can make is in yourself.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Someone's sitting in the shade today because someone planted a tree a long time ago.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: It takes 20 years to build a reputation and five minutes to ruin it.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: In the business world, the rearview mirror is always clearer than the windshield.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: The difference between successful people and really successful people is that really successful people say no to almost everything.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Investing is not a game where the guy with the 160 IQ beats the guy with 130 IQ. Rationality is essential.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Invert, always invert. Turn a situation or problem upside down. Look at it backward.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: All I want to know is where I'm going to die, so I'll never go there.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: The big money is not in the buying and selling, but in the waiting.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: A great business at a fair price is superior to a fair business at a great price.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: Spend each day trying to be a little wiser than you were when you woke up.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: The iron rule of nature is: you get what you reward for. If you want ants to come, you put sugar on the floor.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: Knowing what you don't know is more useful than being brilliant.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: People are trying to be smart. All I am trying to do is not to be idiotic, but it's harder than most people think.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: Take a simple idea and take it seriously.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: The best thing a human being can do is to help another human being know more.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: You must force yourself to consider opposing arguments. Especially when they challenge your best-loved ideas.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: The world is not driven by greed. It's driven by envy.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: Three things ruin people: drugs, liquor, and leverage.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: The individual investor should act consistently as an investor and not as a speculator.
principles.author: Benjamin Graham

principles.text: In the short run, the market is a voting machine but in the long run, it is a weighing machine.
principles.author: Benjamin Graham

principles.text: The margin of safety is always dependent on the price paid.
principles.author: Benjamin Graham

principles.text: The intelligent investor is a realist who sells to optimists and buys from pessimists.
principles.author: Benjamin Graham

principles.text: Have the courage of your knowledge and experience. If you have formed a conclusion from the facts, act on it.
principles.author: Benjamin Graham

principles.text: The investor's chief problem — and even his worst enemy — is likely to be himself.
principles.author: Benjamin Graham

principles.text: To achieve satisfactory investment results is easier than most people realize; to achieve superior results is harder than it looks.
principles.author: Benjamin Graham

principles.text: The essence of investment management is the management of risks, not the management of returns.
principles.author: Benjamin Graham

principles.text: Buy not on optimism, but on arithmetic.
principles.author: Benjamin Graham

principles.text: An investment operation is one which, upon thorough analysis, promises safety of principal and an adequate return.
principles.author: Benjamin Graham

principles.text: The stock market is filled with individuals who know the price of everything, but the value of nothing.
principles.author: Philip Fisher

principles.text: If the job has been correctly done when a common stock is purchased, the time to sell it is — almost never.
principles.author: Philip Fisher

principles.text: I don't want a lot of good investments; I want a few outstanding ones.
principles.author: Philip Fisher

principles.text: The best time to sell a great stock is almost never.
principles.author: Philip Fisher

principles.text: Conservative investors sleep well.
principles.author: Philip Fisher

principles.text: Go on the scuttlebutt method — talk to customers, suppliers, competitors, and former employees.
principles.author: Philip Fisher

principles.text: Is the company making above-average efforts to develop new products or services that will further increase sales?
principles.author: Philip Fisher

principles.text: Know what you own, and know why you own it.
principles.author: Peter Lynch

principles.text: The key to making money in stocks is not to get scared out of them.
principles.author: Peter Lynch

principles.text: Go for a business that any idiot can run — because sooner or later, any idiot probably is going to run it.
principles.author: Peter Lynch

principles.text: In this business, if you're good, you're right six times out of ten.
principles.author: Peter Lynch

principles.text: Behind every stock is a company. Find out what it's doing.
principles.author: Peter Lynch

principles.text: The person that turns over the most rocks wins the game.
principles.author: Peter Lynch

principles.text: Invest in what you know. Your edge is not something you get from Wall Street.
principles.author: Peter Lynch

principles.text: Far more money has been lost by investors preparing for corrections than has been lost in corrections themselves.
principles.author: Peter Lynch

principles.text: Time is on your side when you own shares of superior companies.
principles.author: Peter Lynch

principles.text: The trick is not to learn to trust your gut feelings, but rather to discipline yourself to ignore them.
principles.author: Peter Lynch

principles.text: The most important thing is being aware of what you don't know, and the most dangerous thing is thinking you know more than you do.
principles.author: Howard Marks

principles.text: You can't predict. You can prepare.
principles.author: Howard Marks

principles.text: The biggest investing errors come not from factors that are informational or analytical, but from those that are psychological.
principles.author: Howard Marks

principles.text: There are old investors, and there are bold investors, but there are no old bold investors.
principles.author: Howard Marks

principles.text: Investment success doesn't come from buying good things, but rather from buying things well.
principles.author: Howard Marks

principles.text: Being too far ahead of your time is indistinguishable from being wrong.
principles.author: Howard Marks

principles.text: The most profitable investment actions are by definition contrarian: you buy when everyone else is selling and sell when everyone else is buying.
principles.author: Howard Marks

principles.text: Experience is what you got when you didn't get what you wanted.
principles.author: Howard Marks

principles.text: Move forward, but with caution.
principles.author: Howard Marks

principles.text: The second-level thinker takes many things into account: What is the range of likely future outcomes? Which outcome do I think will occur?
principles.author: Howard Marks

principles.text: He who lives by the crystal ball will eat shattered glass.
principles.author: Ray Dalio

principles.text: Diversifying well is the most important thing you need to do in order to invest well.
principles.author: Ray Dalio

principles.text: Pain plus reflection equals progress.
principles.author: Ray Dalio

principles.text: If you're not failing, you're not pushing your limits, and if you're not pushing your limits, you're not maximizing your potential.
principles.author: Ray Dalio

principles.text: Don't let fears of what others think of you stand in your way.
principles.author: Ray Dalio

principles.text: The biggest mistake investors make is to believe that what happened in the recent past is likely to persist.
principles.author: Ray Dalio

principles.text: Every time you confront something painful, you are at a potentially important juncture in your life.
principles.author: Ray Dalio

principles.text: Think about what the risks are of being wrong versus the rewards of being right.
principles.author: Ray Dalio

principles.text: There is nothing wrong with making mistakes. What's wrong is not learning from them.
principles.author: Ray Dalio

principles.text: The Holy Grail of investing is to find 15 or more good, uncorrelated return streams.
principles.author: Ray Dalio

principles.text: The four most dangerous words in investing are: 'This time it's different.'
principles.author: John Templeton

principles.text: Bull markets are born on pessimism, grow on skepticism, mature on optimism, and die on euphoria.
principles.author: John Templeton

principles.text: To buy when others are despondently selling and to sell when others are avidly buying requires the greatest fortitude.
principles.author: John Templeton

principles.text: The time of maximum pessimism is the best time to buy, and the time of maximum optimism is the best time to sell.
principles.author: John Templeton

principles.text: Compound interest is the eighth wonder of the world. He who understands it, earns it; he who doesn't, pays it.
principles.author: Albert Einstein

principles.text: In investing, what is comfortable is rarely profitable.
principles.author: Robert Arnott

principles.text: The stock market is the only market where things go on sale and all the customers run out of the store.
principles.author: Cullen Roche

principles.text: Do not take yearly results too seriously. Instead, focus on four- or five-year averages.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Beware the investment activity that produces applause; the great moves are usually greeted by yawns.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: It is not necessary to do extraordinary things to get extraordinary results.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: The most important quality for an investor is temperament, not intellect.
principles.author: Warren Buffett

principles.text: Mimicking the herd invites regression to the mean.
principles.author: Charlie Munger

principles.text: Patience is the secret to making money in the stock market.
principles.author: William J. O'Neil
principles.source: How to Make Money in Stocks

principles.text: The goal of a successful trader is to make the best trades. Money is secondary.
principles.author: Alexander Elder

principles.text: Markets can remain irrational longer than you can remain solvent.
principles.author: John Maynard Keynes

principles.text: The secret to investing is to figure out the value of something and then pay a lot less.
principles.author: Joel Greenblatt
principles.source: The Little Book That Beats the Market

principles.text: Returns matter a lot. It's our capital.
principles.author: Seth Klarman

principles.text: The financial markets generally are unpredictable. So that one has to have different scenarios.
principles.author: George Soros

principles.text: It's not whether you're right or wrong that's important, but how much money you make when you're right and how much you lose when you're wrong.
principles.author: George Soros

principles.text: An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.
principles.author: Benjamin Franklin
